newsBriefing: It was a whirlwind Monday across the sports world, headlined by a massive reshuffling of the NFL’s quarterback landscape as the Falcons agreed to a one-year deal with Tua Tagovailoa following his release from the Dolphins, who quickly pivoted to sign Malik Willis to a three-year, $67.5 million contract. The free agency frenzy continued with the Raiders making Tyler Linderbaum the highest-paid interior lineman in history on an $81 million deal, while the Panthers bolstered their defense by landing second-team All-Pro linebacker Devin Lloyd for $45 million and the Steelers added physical runner Rico Dowdle. On the hardwood, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander delivered a legendary performance by scoring 35 points to match Wilt Chamberlain’s record of 126 consecutive 20-point games, punctuated by a game-winning buzzer-beater to sink the Nuggets. Emotional storylines emerged elsewhere as former U.S. Open champion Gary Woodland bravely went public with his PTSD diagnosis following brain surgery, and UFC legend Jon Jones requested his release from the promotion amid a heated public dispute with Dana White over a missed opportunity to fight at the White House. Meanwhile, the road to March Madness gained clarity as Troy and Furman officially punched their tickets to the NCAA Tournament by securing conference titles on an action-packed night of mid-major hoops.
